{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 久比岐国造 |家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[頸城氏]] |家祖 = [[御戈命]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[地祇]]） |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = [[越後国]] |人物 = [[#人物]]参照 |支流 = [[青海氏]]（首） }} '''久比岐国造'''（くびきのくにのみやつこ・くびきこくぞう）は、[[#支配領域|久比岐国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 先祖 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]の御代に'''[[倭国造|大和直]]'''と同祖の'''御戈命'''を国造に任命したとされる。 *『諸系譜』第二冊所載の「大和宿祢及海直」系図では'''[[珍彦|珍彦命]]'''の6世孫・[[市磯長尾市]]の弟が御戈命であるとしており<ref>[[中田憲信]]「大和宿祢及海直」『諸系譜』第二冊。</ref>、『百家系図稿』巻二十一の系図では珍彦命の5世孫・御物宿禰の弟が御戈命としている<ref>[[鈴木真年]]『百家系図稿』巻二十一。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[頸城氏]]'''（くびきうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。後裔に[[青海氏]]（おうみうじ、姓は[[首 (姓)|首]]）がおり、[[海人族]]の[[倭国造]]と同族。青海氏は『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に椎根津彦命の後裔とある<ref>「右京神別 青海首」『新撰姓氏録』、弘仁六年。</ref>。この一族に関連して、同国内の[[蒲原郡]]にも青海郷が存在する。これに対して頸城直氏が史書に見えないのは、久比岐国造が衰滅したか、早くに蒲原郡など他地域に移遷したなどの理由が考えられる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第二章 越と阿倍氏族」『越と出雲の夜明け』法令出版、2008年、76頁。</ref>。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''久比岐国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[越後国]]'''[[頸城郡]]'''西部、現在の[[糸魚川市]]に相当する。
[[古墳]]の築造状況や深江の地名などから、[[高志深江国造]]を[[蒲原郡]]や[[沼垂郡]]深江に想定し、久比岐国造の本拠を[[上越市]]・[[妙高市]]を中心域に比定する説がある。しかし大和川や青海川、[[青海町]]、青海神社など国造に関係した地名が糸魚川市に多く見られる一方、上越市や妙高市にはこれら地名が見られない。また深江の地名が頸城郡内にも存在したと見られ、郡内に高志深江国造の後裔氏族が居住していたことなどから、久比岐国造の本拠は糸魚川市に比定する方が妥当であるとする説もある<ref>宝賀寿男「第二章 越と阿倍氏族」『越と出雲の夜明け』法令出版、2008年、70-73頁。</ref>。
== 氏神 == [[式内社]]の'''[[青海神社 (糸魚川市)|青海神社]]'''か。国造の[[祖神]]である[[椎根津彦命]]を祀る。
=== 関連神社 === *[[大神社 (糸魚川市平)|大神社]]（おおみわのかむやしろ） *:[[糸魚川市]]大字平に鎮座する式内論社。宮司である佐藤氏の遠祖である美保古命（御戈命）を祀る。
== 子孫 == *[[青海夫人勾子]] *: [[古墳時代]]の人物で[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の女官。天皇が出仕しない[[大伴金村]]のもとへ遣わした。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[越後国]] *[[糸魚川市]] *[[高志深江国造]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:くびきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:頸城氏|*]] [[Category:越後国]] [[Category:新潟県の歴史]]

=== 関連神社 === *[[大神社 (糸魚川市平)|大神社]]（おおみわのかむやしろ） *:[[糸魚川市]]大字平に鎮座する大神社の式内論社。宮司である佐藤氏の遠祖である美保古命（御戈命）を祀る。 *[[青海神社 (加茂市)|青海神社]]（おうみじんじゃ） *:[[加茂市]]に鎮座する青海神社の式内論社。椎根津彦命と[[倭大国魂神|大國魂神]]を祀る。
== 子孫 == *[[青海夫人勾子]] *: [[古墳時代]]の人物で[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の女官。天皇が出仕しない[[大伴金村]]のもとへ遣わした。 == 系譜 == {{ワタツミの子孫}}
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[越後国]] *[[糸魚川市]] *[[高志深江国造]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:くびきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:頸城氏|*]] [[Category:越後国]] [[Category:新潟県の歴史]]

revision.comment: 戻し：典拠不明であり掲載不適当と判断。
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 久比岐国造 |家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[頸城氏]] |家祖 = [[御戈命]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[地祇]]） |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = [[越後国]] |人物 = [[#人物]]参照 |支流 = [[青海氏]]（首） }} '''久比岐国造'''（くびきのくにのみやつこ・くびきこくぞう）は、[[#支配領域|久比岐国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 先祖 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]の御代に'''[[倭国造|大和直]]'''と同祖の'''御戈命'''を国造に任命したとされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[頸城氏]]'''（くびきうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。後裔に[[青海氏]]（おうみうじ、姓は[[首 (姓)|首]]）がおり、[[倭国造]]と同族。青海氏は『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に椎根津彦命の後裔とある<ref>「右京神別 青海首」『新撰姓氏録』、弘仁六年。</ref>。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''久比岐国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[越後国]]'''[[頸城郡]]'''西部、現在の[[糸魚川市]]に相当する。
[[古墳]]の築造状況や深江の地名などから、[[高志深江国造]]を[[蒲原郡]]や[[沼垂郡]]深江に想定し、久比岐国造の本拠を[[上越市]]・[[妙高市]]を中心域に比定される{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
宝賀寿男は、大和川や青海川、[[青海町]]、青海神社など国造に関係した地名が糸魚川市に多く見られる一方、上越市や妙高市にはこれら地名が見られない。また深江の地名が頸城郡内にも存在したと見られ、郡内に高志深江国造の後裔氏族が居住していたことなどから、久比岐国造の本拠は糸魚川市に比定する方が妥当であると主張している<ref>宝賀寿男「第二章 越と阿倍氏族」『越と出雲の夜明け』法令出版、2008年、70-73頁。</ref>。
== 氏神 == [[式内社]]の'''[[青海神社 (糸魚川市)|青海神社]]'''か。国造の[[祖神]]である[[椎根津彦命]]を祀る。
=== 関連神社 === *[[大神社 (糸魚川市平)|大神社]]（おおみわのかむやしろ） *:[[糸魚川市]]大字平に鎮座する大神社の式内論社。宮司である佐藤氏の遠祖である美保古命（御戈命）を祀る。 *[[青海神社 (加茂市)|青海神社]]（おうみじんじゃ） *:[[加茂市]]に鎮座する青海神社の式内論社。椎根津彦命と[[倭大国魂神|大國魂神]]を祀る。
== 子孫 == *[[青海夫人勾子]] *: [[古墳時代]]の人物で[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の女官。天皇が出仕しない[[大伴金村]]のもとへ遣わした。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[越後国]] *[[糸魚川市]] *[[高志深江国造]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:くびきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:頸城氏|*]] [[Category:越後国]] [[Category:新潟県の歴史]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''{{subst:PAGENAME}}''' is a descendant of Watatsumi. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]]"
revision.text:
'''Kubiki Kokuzo''' is a descendant of Watatsumi. See the family tree below for more info.
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | clan_name = Kubiki Kokuzō | crest = <!-- Image of family crest --> | crest_caption = | ancestral_name = [[Kubiki clan]] | founder = [[Mikohari no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]], earthly deity) | origin = [[Yamato Province]] | stronghold = [[Echigo Province]] | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = [[Ōmi clan]] (首, "Omi-no-Kubi") }}
'''Kubiki Kokuzō''' (久比岐国造, "Governor of Kubiki Province") was the title of the ruler of the ancient [[Kubiki Province]].
== Overview == ### Ancestors According to the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Kokuzō Hongi"), [[Mikohari no Mikoto]], a relative of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], was appointed as the first Kubiki Kokuzō during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]].
### Clan The '''[[Kubiki clan]]''' (久比岐氏, Kubiki-uji), bearing the title ''Naokubi'' (直, "Na"), is described as descendants of Mikohari no Mikoto. The [[Ōmi clan]] (青海氏), a branch of the Kubiki clan, is identified in the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' as descendants of [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]].<ref>"Sakyō Jinbetsu: Omi no Kubi" in the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku,'' Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Domain == ### Territory The Kubiki Kokuzō ruled the region historically known as '''Kubiki Province''', corresponding to the western part of [[Kubiki District]] in [[Echigo Province]] and present-day [[Itoigawa City]].
Based on archaeological findings, including burial mounds and the place name "Fukue," scholars propose that the [[Koshi Fukue Kokuzō]] was based in [[Kambara District]] or [[Numata District]]. Hoga Toshio argues that since Itoigawa features numerous names linked to the Kubiki Kokuzō, such as Ōmi River, Ōmi Shrine, and Ōmi Town, while such names are absent in [[Jōetsu City]] and [[Myōkō City]], Itoigawa is more likely the original base of the Kubiki Kokuzō.<ref>Hoga Toshio, "Chapter 2: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Clan Shrine == The '''[[Ōmi Shrine (Itoigawa City)|Ōmi Shrine]]''' in [[Itoigawa City]] may be the clan shrine. It enshrines [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Kubiki Kokuzō.
### Related Shrines * [[Ōmi Shrine (Kamoshi)|Ōmi Shrine]] (Kamoshi) : Located in [[Kamo City]], this shrine is dedicated to [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]] and [[Yamato no Ōkunidama no Kami]]. * [[Ōgami Shrine (Itoigawa City Hei)|Ōgami Shrine]] : Located in Hei, Itoigawa City, this shrine is associated with the Kokuzō and enshrines [[Mikohari no Mikoto]].
== Descendants == * [[Ōmi Consort Kōshi]] : A figure of the [[Kofun period]] who served as a court lady during the reign of [[Emperor Kinmei]]. She was sent by the Emperor to serve [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] when he refused to attend court.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Echigo Province]] * [[Itoigawa City]] * [[Koshi Fukue Kokuzō]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kubiki Kokuzō}} [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:Kubiki clan|*]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Niigata Prefecture]]
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[Category:Original pages]]

== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Echigo Province]] * [[Itoigawa City]] * [[Koshi Fukue Kokuzō]]

'''Kubiki Kokuzō''' (久比岐国造, "Governor of Kubiki Province") was the title of the ruler of the ancient [[Kubiki Province]].
== Overview == ### Ancestors According to the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Kokuzō Hongi"), [[Mikohari no Mikoto]], a relative of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], was appointed as the first Kubiki Kokuzō during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]].
### Clan The '''[[Kubiki clan]]''' (久比岐氏, Kubiki-uji), bearing the title ''Naokubi'' (直, "Na"), is described as descendants of Mikohari no Mikoto. The [[Ōmi clan]] (青海氏), a branch of the Kubiki clan, is identified in the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' as descendants of [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]].<ref>"Sakyō Jinbetsu: Omi no Kubi" in the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku,'' Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Domain == ### Territory The Kubiki Kokuzō ruled the region historically known as '''Kubiki Province''', corresponding to the western part of [[Kubiki District]] in [[Echigo Province]] and present-day [[Itoigawa City]].
Based on archaeological findings, including burial mounds and the place name "Fukue," scholars propose that the [[Koshi Fukue Kokuzō]] was based in [[Kambara District]] or [[Numata District]]. Hoga Toshio argues that since Itoigawa features numerous names linked to the Kubiki Kokuzō, such as Ōmi River, Ōmi Shrine, and Ōmi Town, while such names are absent in [[Jōetsu City]] and [[Myōkō City]], Itoigawa is more likely the original base of the Kubiki Kokuzō.<ref>Hoga Toshio, "Chapter 2: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Clan Shrine == The '''[[Ōmi Shrine (Itoigawa City)|Ōmi Shrine]]''' in [[Itoigawa City]] may be the clan shrine. It enshrines [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Kubiki Kokuzō.
### Related Shrines * [[Ōmi Shrine (Kamoshi)|Ōmi Shrine]] (Kamoshi) : Located in [[Kamo City]], this shrine is dedicated to [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]] and [[Yamato no Ōkunidama no Kami]]. * [[Ōgami Shrine (Itoigawa City Hei)|Ōgami Shrine]] : Located in Hei, Itoigawa City, this shrine is associated with the Kokuzō and enshrines [[Mikohari no Mikoto]].
== Descendants == * [[Ōmi Consort Kōshi]] : A figure of the [[Kofun period]] who served as a court lady during the reign of [[Emperor Kinmei]]. She was sent by the Emperor to serve [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] when he refused to attend court.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * [[Echigo Province]] * [[Itoigawa City]] * [[Koshi Fukue Kokuzō]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | clan_name = Kubiki Kokuzō | crest = <!-- Image of family crest --> | crest_caption = | ancestral_name = [[Kubiki clan]] | founder = [[Mikohari no Mikoto]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]], earthly deity) | origin = [[Yamato Province]] | stronghold = [[Echigo Province]] | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] | branches = [[Ōmi clan]] (首, "Omi-no-Kubi") }}
'''Kubiki Kokuzō''' (久比岐国造, "Governor of Kubiki Province") was the title of the ruler of the ancient [[Kubiki Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the ''[[Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Kokuzō Hongi"), [[Mikohari no Mikoto]], a relative of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], was appointed as the first Kubiki Kokuzō during the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''[[Kubiki clan]]''' (久比岐氏, Kubiki-uji), bearing the title ''Naokubi'' (直, "Na"), is described as descendants of Mikohari no Mikoto. The [[Ōmi clan]] (青海氏), a branch of the Kubiki clan, is identified in the ''[[Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' as descendants of [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]].<ref>"Sakyō Jinbetsu: Omi no Kubi" in the ''Shinsen Shōjiroku,'' Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Domain ==
=== Territory === The Kubiki Kokuzō ruled the region historically known as '''Kubiki Province''', corresponding to the western part of [[Kubiki District]] in [[Echigo Province]] and present-day [[Itoigawa City]].
Based on archaeological findings, including burial mounds and the place name "Fukue," scholars propose that the [[Koshi Fukue Kokuzō]] was based in [[Kambara District]] or [[Numata District]]. Hoga Toshio argues that since Itoigawa features numerous names linked to the Kubiki Kokuzō, such as Ōmi River, Ōmi Shrine, and Ōmi Town, while such names are absent in [[Jōetsu City]] and [[Myōkō City]], Itoigawa is more likely the original base of the Kubiki Kokuzō.<ref>Hoga Toshio, "Chapter 2: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Clan Shrine == The '''[[Ōmi Shrine (Itoigawa City)|Ōmi Shrine]]''' in [[Itoigawa City]] may be the clan shrine. It enshrines [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]], the ancestor of the Kubiki Kokuzō.
=== Related Shrines === * [[Ōmi Shrine (Kamoshi)|Ōmi Shrine]] (Kamoshi) : Located in [[Kamo City]], this shrine is dedicated to [[Shiine Tsuhiko no Mikoto]] and [[Yamato no Ōkunidama no Kami]]. * [[Ōgami Shrine (Itoigawa City Hei)|Ōgami Shrine]] : Located in Hei, Itoigawa City, this shrine is associated with the Kokuzō and enshrines [[Mikohari no Mikoto]].
== Descendants == * [[Ōmi Consort Kōshi]] : A figure of the [[Kofun period]] who served as a court lady during the reign of [[Emperor Kinmei]]. She was sent by the Emperor to serve [[Ōtomo no Kanamura]] when he refused to attend court.

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' * Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' * Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' * A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' * Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' * A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}
[[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kubiki clan]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Niigata Prefecture]] [[ja:久比岐国造]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kubiki clan|*]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[ja:久比岐国造]]

However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}
[[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kubiki clan]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Niigata Prefecture]] [[ja:久比岐国造]] [[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kubiki Kokuzo-qq]] [[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kubiki clan|*]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kubiki Kokuzo-qq]] [[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:kk]] ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References=== [[ja:久比岐国造]]

=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.

It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}

'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}
