revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造}}
<!--Kubiki Kokuzo-->
<!--Kubiki Kokuzo-->
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=draft}} <!--Kubiki Kokuzo-->

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=draft}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=draft}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=draft}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|12=draft}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=draft}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=draft}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=draft}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=draft}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=draft}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造|12=draft}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=draft}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市|12=draft}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=draft}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市|12=draft}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=draft}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=draft}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=draft}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=draft}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=draft}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市|12=draft}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=draft}} <!--Kubiki Kokuzo-->
<!--Kubiki Kokuzo-->
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= <!-- Image of the clan crest --> |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=<!-- Image of the clan crest -->|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=draft}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=draft}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=draft}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|12=draft}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=draft}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=draft}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=draft}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=draft}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=draft}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造|12=draft}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=draft}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市|12=draft}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=draft}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市|12=draft}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=draft}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=draft}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=draft}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=draft}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=draft}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市|12=draft}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市|12=draft}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=draft}} <!--Kubiki Kokuzo-->
<!--Kubiki Kokuzo-->
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kubiki clan]] [[Category:Echigo Province]] [[Category:History of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Kubiki Kokuzo]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kubiki clan|*]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kubiki Kokuzo]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:久比岐国造]] [[sn:Kubiki Kokuzo]] [[en:Kubiki Kokuzo]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kubiki Kokuzo}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:久比岐国造 up to rev 98569776
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 久比岐国造 |家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 --> |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[頸城氏]] |家祖 = [[御戈命]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[地祇]]） |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = [[越後国]] |人物 = [[#人物]]参照 |支流 = [[青海氏]]（首） }} '''久比岐国造'''（くびきのくにのみやつこ・くびきこくぞう）は、[[#支配領域|久比岐国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 先祖 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では、[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]の御代に'''[[倭国造|大和直]]'''と同祖の'''御戈命'''を国造に任命したとされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[頸城氏]]'''（くびきうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。後裔に[[青海氏]]（おうみうじ、姓は[[首 (姓)|首]]）がおり、[[倭国造]]と同族。青海氏は『[[新撰姓氏録]]』に椎根津彦命の後裔とある<ref>「右京神別 青海首」『新撰姓氏録』、弘仁六年。</ref>。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''久比岐国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[越後国]]'''[[頸城郡]]'''西部、現在の[[糸魚川市]]に相当する。
[[古墳]]の築造状況や深江の地名などから、[[高志深江国造]]を[[蒲原郡]]や[[沼垂郡]]深江に想定し、久比岐国造の本拠を[[上越市]]・[[妙高市]]を中心域に比定される{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
宝賀寿男は、大和川や青海川、[[青海町]]、青海神社など国造に関係した地名が糸魚川市に多く見られる一方、上越市や妙高市にはこれら地名が見られない。また深江の地名が頸城郡内にも存在したと見られ、郡内に高志深江国造の後裔氏族が居住していたことなどから、久比岐国造の本拠は糸魚川市に比定する方が妥当であると主張している<ref>宝賀寿男「第二章 越と阿倍氏族」『越と出雲の夜明け』法令出版、2008年、70-73頁。</ref>。
== 氏神 == [[式内社]]の'''[[青海神社 (糸魚川市)|青海神社]]'''か。国造の[[祖神]]である[[椎根津彦命]]を祀る。
=== 関連神社 === *[[大神社 (糸魚川市平)|大神社]]（おおみわのかむやしろ） *:[[糸魚川市]]大字平に鎮座する大神社の式内論社。宮司である佐藤氏の遠祖である美保古命（御戈命）を祀る。 *[[青海神社 (加茂市)|青海神社]]（おうみじんじゃ） *:[[加茂市]]に鎮座する青海神社の式内論社。椎根津彦命と[[倭大国魂神|大國魂神]]を祀る。
== 子孫 == *[[青海夫人勾子]] *: [[古墳時代]]の人物で[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の女官。天皇が出仕しない[[大伴金村]]のもとへ遣わした。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[越後国]] *[[糸魚川市]] *[[高志深江国造]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:くびきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:頸城氏|*]] [[Category:越後国]] [[Category:新潟県の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[久比岐国造]] to [[Kubiki Kokuzo]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

宝賀寿男は、大和川や青海川、[[青海町]]、青海神社など国造に関係した地名が糸魚川市に多く見られる一方、上越市や妙高市にはこれら地名が見られない。また深江の地名が頸城郡内にも存在したと見られ、郡内に高志深江国造の後裔氏族が居住していたことなどから、久比岐国造の本拠は糸魚川市に比定する方が妥当であると主張している<ref>宝賀寿男「第二章 越と阿倍氏族」『越と出雲の夜明け』法令出版、2008年、70-73頁。</ref>。
== 氏神 == [[式内社]]の'''[[青海神社 (糸魚川市)|青海神社]]'''か。国造の[[祖神]]である[[椎根津彦命]]を祀る。
=== 関連神社 === *[[大神社 (糸魚川市平)|大神社]]（おおみわのかむやしろ） *:[[糸魚川市]]大字平に鎮座する大神社の式内論社。宮司である佐藤氏の遠祖である美保古命（御戈命）を祀る。 *[[青海神社 (加茂市)|青海神社]]（おうみじんじゃ） *:[[加茂市]]に鎮座する青海神社の式内論社。椎根津彦命と[[倭大国魂神|大國魂神]]を祀る。
== 子孫 == *[[青海夫人勾子]] *: [[古墳時代]]の人物で[[欽明天皇|欽明朝]]の女官。天皇が出仕しない[[大伴金村]]のもとへ遣わした。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[越後国]] *[[糸魚川市]] *[[高志深江国造]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:くびきのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:国造]] [[Category:頸城氏|*]] [[Category:越後国]] [[Category:新潟県の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|qq=draft}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|qq=draft}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|qq=draft}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|qq=draft}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造|qq=draft}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|qq=draft}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市|qq=draft}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市|qq=draft}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|qq=draft}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市|qq=draft}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===
{{translated page|ja|久比岐国造|version=98569776|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Kubiki clan|*]]
}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|qq=}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|qq=}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|qq=}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa, Niigata|ja|糸魚川市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoigawa, Niigata|qq=}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu|ja|上越市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōetsu|qq=}} or {{ill|Myōkō|ja|妙高市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōkō|qq=}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|qq=}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|qq=}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|qq=}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|qq=}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo|ja|加茂市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo|qq=}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|ja|大伴金村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Kanamura|qq=}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|ja|越後国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoigawa|qq=}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|qq=}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|qq=}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|qq=}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|qq=}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|qq=}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|qq=}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|qq=}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|qq=}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|qq=}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|qq=}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|qq=}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|qq=}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|qq=}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|qq=}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|qq=}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|qq=}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|qq=}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|qq=}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|qq=}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|qq=}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|qq=|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|qq=|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|qq=|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|qq=|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|qq=|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|qq=|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|qq=|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|qq=|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===
