<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland=[[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]|Base=[[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]]|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]].
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Miyatsuko]]. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa, Niigata]].
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Jōetsu]] or [[:en:Myōkō, Niigata|Myōkō]]. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in [[:en:Kamo, Niigata|Kamo]], it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], she served in the court of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] and was sent as a representative to [[:en:Ōtomo no Kanamura|Ōtomo no Kanamura]].
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Echigo Province|Echigo Province]] * [[:en:Itoigawa|Itoigawa]] * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.

They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11387078|lt=Yamato no Miyatsuko}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>

=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa, Niigata}}.

However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97383587]] Wikidata name: Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11387078|lt=Yamato no Miyatsuko}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa, Niigata}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu}} or {{ill|Myōkō, Niigata|en|Myōkō, Niigata|WD=Q279642|lt=Myōkō}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo, Niigata|en|Kamo, Niigata|WD=Q857293|lt=Kamo}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|en|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358|lt=Ōtomo no Kanamura}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|en|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358|lt=Ōtomo no Kanamura}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]]
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11387078|lt=Yamato no Miyatsuko}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa, Niigata}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu}} or {{ill|Myōkō, Niigata|en|Myōkō, Niigata|WD=Q279642|lt=Myōkō}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo, Niigata|en|Kamo, Niigata|WD=Q857293|lt=Kamo}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|en|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358|lt=Ōtomo no Kanamura}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===

== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo, Niigata|en|Kamo, Niigata|WD=Q857293|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo, Niigata}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|en|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358|lt=Ōtomo no Kanamura|1=Ōtomo no Kanamura}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa|1=Itoigawa}} * {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:久比岐国造]]
{{Japanese clan |Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko |Crest= |CrestName= |Origin=[[Kubiki clan]] |Founder=[[Mikoh no Mikoto]] |Type=[[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]]) |Homeland=[[Yamato Province]] |Base=[[Echigo Province]] |NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]] |Branches=[[Aomi clan]] (首) }}{{Japanese clan|Name=Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Crest=|CrestName=|Origin={{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Founder={{ill|Mikoh no Mikoto|ja|御戈命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoh no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Type={{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Chigi|ja|地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}})|Homeland={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}|Base={{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}|NotableMembers=See [[#Notable Members]]|Branches={{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (首)}}
'''Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (久比岐国造, also pronounced ''Kubiki Kokuzō'') governed the [[#Territory|territory of Kubiki Province]] as a [[Kuni no Miyatsuko]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11387078|lt=Yamato no Miyatsuko|1=Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|WD=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa, Niigata|1=Itoigawa}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}} or {{ill|Myōkō, Niigata|en|Myōkō, Niigata|WD=Q279642|lt=Myōkō|1=Myōkō, Niigata}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo, Niigata|en|Kamo, Niigata|WD=Q857293|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo, Niigata}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|en|Ōtomo no Kanamura|WD=Q11432358|lt=Ōtomo no Kanamura|1=Ōtomo no Kanamura}}.

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' (Chronicles of Ancient Matters), the ''{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}'' records that '''Mikoh no Mikoto''' (御戈命), a relative of the ''{{ill|Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja|大和直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', was appointed as the Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}.
=== Clan === The '''{{ill|Kubiki clan|ja|頸城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Kubiki Uji'') bore the honorary title ''Na'' (直). Descendants include the '''{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (also pronounced ''Ōmi Uji''), whose members held the honorary title ''Kubi'' (首). They are considered kin to the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11387078|lt=Yamato no Miyatsuko|1=Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}. The ''Aomi clan'' is listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} as descendants of ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' (椎根津彦命).<ref>"Ukyō Shinbetsu Aomi no Kubi," ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kōnin 6 (815).</ref>
== Headquarters ==
=== Territory === The Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as '''Kubiki Province''' at the time, which corresponds to the western part of the '''{{ill|Kubiki District, Echigo Province|ja|頸城郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki District, Echigo Province|1=Kubiki district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664982}}''' in modern-day {{ill|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|qid=Q857208|lt=Itoigawa, Niigata|1=Itoigawa}}.
Based on the construction of [[kofun]] and place names such as ''Fukae,'' it has been theorized that the {{ill|Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|高志深江国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Fukae Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} resided in the ''Kamihara'' and ''Numata'' districts, while the Kubiki no Miyatsuko's center of influence was likely in the regions of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|qid=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}} or {{ill|Myōkō, Niigata|en|Myōkō, Niigata|qid=Q279642|lt=Myōkō|1=Myōkō, Niigata}}. However, historian Hōga Toshio argues for a base in Itoigawa, citing place names such as ''Yamatogawa'' and ''Aomigawa'' and the prevalence of related names and shrines in the city.<ref>Hōga Toshio, "Chapter Two: Koshi and the Abe Clan," ''The Dawn of Koshi and Izumo,'' Hōrei Publishing, 2008, pp. 70–73.</ref>
== Deity == The clan deity is thought to be worshipped at the {{ill|Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|ja|青海神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Itoigawa)|1=Ōmi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30922754}} in Itoigawa, an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} dedicated to {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|椎根津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 椎根津彦|1=Saonetsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11120574}}.
=== Related Shrines === * '''{{ill|Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja|大神社 (糸魚川市平)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōjinja (Itoigawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' ** Located in the {{ill|Taira area|ja|平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira area|1=平|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97368918}} of Itoigawa, it is considered a candidate for the original shrine. It enshrines ''Mihoko no Mikoto'' (美保古命), the clan ancestor. * '''{{ill|Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|ja|青海神社 (加茂市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Shrine (Kamo)|1=Aomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q112923996}}''' ** Located in {{ill|Kamo, Niigata|en|Kamo, Niigata|qid=Q857293|lt=Kamo|1=Kamo, Niigata}}, it is another potential candidate. It enshrines both ''Shiinetsuhiko no Mikoto'' and ''Ōkunitama no Kami'' (大國魂神).
== Descendants == * '''Aomi Consort (Kōshi)''' ** A prominent figure during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, she served in the court of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} and was sent as a representative to {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|en|Ōtomo no Kanamura|qid=Q11432358|lt=Ōtomo no Kanamura|1=Ōtomo no Kanamura}}.
